mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real GDP compresses equity risk premiums by confirming corporate earnings expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in quarters with positive GDP growth are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 4.0 percent signals restrictive monetary policy that increases discount rates and suppresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or the signal exhibits no predictive power in a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent rises in nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm labor market tightening, which boosts consumer spending and corporate revenues, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy over three consecutive rolling 12-month periods, or the interaction term shows a statistically insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and strong aggregate supply, which supports corporate margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of months where INDPRO rises month-over-month, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts reflect improving consumer confidence and credit availability, which stimulates related economic activity and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 20-day forward returns following months of rising HOUST are statistically indistinguishable from returns following months of falling HOUST (p-value > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 5.5 percent signals full employment, which reduces wage-driven inflation fears and allows equity multiples to expand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward analysis shows no significant difference in 20-day forward returns between months where UNRATE crosses below 5.5% versus months where it remains above, or the signal yields negative average returns in the test period.

mechanism_narrative: Diverging trends where retail sales growth decelerates while consumer price inflation accelerates indicate stagflationary pressures that erode real purchasing power and corporate margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 40% of historical stagflation episodes, or forward returns remain positive despite simultaneous decelerating RSAFS and accelerating CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate and the consumer price index signal an active inflation-fighting monetary stance that tightens financial conditions and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal does not produce a negative Rank IC greater than -0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX forward returns are not significantly lower during periods of concurrent rate and inflation hikes.
